questions.question: How much cheaper is thinking off than high thinking?
questions.method: Compare mean run cost across the three off and high audits.
questions.answer: Thinking off averages $0.414, high averages $0.563; off is 26.5% cheaper.

questions.question: How much faster is low thinking than minimal thinking?
questions.method: Compare mean throughput in tokens per second across the three low and minimal audits.
questions.answer: Low averages 151.0 tok/s, minimal averages 159.3 tok/s; low is 5.2% slower.

questions.question: How many raw suggestions did each run produce?
questions.method: Count parsed suggestion lines in each audit.
questions.answer: Runs produced between 7 and 12 raw suggestions.

questions.question: How many unique canonical suggestions did each run cover?
questions.method: Map each raw suggestion to canonical ids and count uniques.
questions.answer: The best single run is gpt-5.5/high/audit_2 with 12 unique canonical suggestions.

questions.question: At k=3, does minimal thinking produce the same canonical coverage as xhigh?
questions.method: For each thinking level, union canonical ids over sorted runs audit_0 through audit_2.
questions.answer: Minimal covers 10 canonical suggestions at k=3; xhigh covers 14.

questions.question: What is the cost per unique addressed suggestion?
questions.method: For each run, divide cost by the number of covered canonical suggestions whose resolution is not not_addressed.
questions.answer: The lowest cost per unique addressed suggestion is gpt-5.5/off/audit_2 at $0.0339.

questions.question: Which suggestions are repeatedly produced but intentionally not addressed?
questions.method: Filter canonical suggestions to not_addressed items with more than one source suggestion.
questions.answer: 5 not-addressed suggestions appeared in more than one audit.

questions.question: Which settings produce more duplicates?
questions.method: Summarize how many raw suggestions map to each canonical suggestion across the full corpus.
questions.answer: The most repeated canonical suggestions have 28 source suggestions.

questions.question: Which settings produce broader suggestions?
questions.method: Count raw suggestions that map to more than one atomic canonical suggestion.
questions.answer: Broad-suggestion rates are reported by thinking level.

questions.question: Which thinking level produced each kind of suggestion?
questions.method: Sum parsed suggestion kinds across runs for each level.
questions.answer: Suggestion kind counts are reported by thinking level.
